LoadSave Sample

The LoadSave sample shows an example implementation of saving games and loading saved games. This implementation meets the certification requirements for memory units (MUs), save game integrity, Xbox MU surprise removal, Xbox MU nicknames, and so on.

Overview

The interface for the sample is based on Xbox usability studies. Players found this interface easy to understand and navigate. It includes visual representations of the Xbox and Xbox MUs, important information about storage devices (such as names and free space), and save game information, including the game graphic, date, and time. Note that the list of saves is sorted from most recent to least recent.

This sample covers many technical issues that you must consider when implementing Load/Save in your game:

  • Validating the save game file(s) with checksums
  • Enumerating MUs on the fly
  • Ensuring that a device has enough room for a saved game
  • Allowing deletion of existing saved games
  • Removal of MU and/or controllers at any time (including while reading/writing data)
  • Ability to scroll lists of saved games
  • Optimal file chunk buffers
  • Displaying progress bars when reading/writing large saves to/from MUs
  • Displaying essential device information and game information

Media Notes

The interface graphics for this sample are used to illustrate the concepts behind loading and saving games and should not be directly used by your game. Use graphics appropriate to the look and feel of your game.

Programming Notes

Lists used by the sample:

  • The MU list includes every possible MU that could be inserted (eight total). Inserted MUs are mounted (IsMounted() == TRUE).
  • The storage device list includes the hard disk and all possible MUs. The hard disk is at position 0 in the list. Storage devices (CXBStorageDevice) provide functions for enumerating saved games.
  • The saved game list is the list of all games on the current storage device. In "Save" mode, the list includes an "empty slot" at the top of the list where new games can be saved.

Indices used by the sample:

  • iCurrDev = the current storage device
  • iCurrGame = the current game in the saved game list
  • iTopGame = the game displayed at the top of the scrollable list
